relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Without love, even angelic speech becomes noise.
Mystery, knowledge, prophecy, and mountain-moving trust are nothing without love.
Even radical giving and bodily surrender gain nothing without love.
Love is patient and kind; it does not envy, boast, or inflate.
Love does not dishonor, self-grasp, flare in irritation, or keep harm-ledgers.
Love refuses injustice and rejoices with truth.
Love bears, trusts, hopes, and endures.
Love does not collapse when gifts pass away.
The complete makes the partial inactive.
Maturity releases childish ways of speaking, thinking, and calculating.
Now perception is mirror and enigma; then face to face, known as fully known.
Trust, hope, and love remain, and love is greatest.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 13 is the governing frequency of the whole letter. Gifts without love are noise; knowledge without love is incompleteness wearing a crown.
